name: Alicante - Elche Coastal Loop
description: This moderate 35.5-mile road cycling route from Alicante to Elxe offers diverse scenery, including the Palmeral of Elche and coastal views.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Central are characterized by a diverse landscape, featuring coastal roads, rolling hills, and challenging mountain passes. The region offers varied terrain, from routes along the Mediterranean coast to ascents into the Sierra de Aitana and other inland mountain ranges. Cyclists can expect a mix of well-maintained roads and scenic climbs, providing opportunities for both endurance training and leisurely rides. Best road cycling routes around Central The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to the port of Tudons and Confrides — Circular from Benidorm, a 48.4 miles (77.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de Confrides and Coll de Rates Loop, a difficult 58.2 miles (93.7 km) path. This route includes two notable mountain passes, offering sustained climbs and descents. Local road cyclists also love the Cabeçó d'Or Viewpoint Loop from Sant Joan d'Alacant, a 33.1 miles (53.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with a significant viewpoint, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Road cycling around Central is defined by coastal roads, challenging mountain passes, and scenic inland valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal loops to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Central are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. More than 55,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Central's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Central?
A: Central offers a wide variety of road cycling routes, with over 380 tours available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including 86 easy, 163 moderate, and 133 difficult options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Central?
A: Road cycling in Central is characterized by a diverse landscape. You'll find routes along the beautiful Mediterranean coast, rolling hills, and challenging ascents into mountain ranges like the Sierra de Aitana. The region offers a mix of well-maintained roads, scenic climbs, and varied terrain suitable for both endurance training and leisurely rides.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Central?
A: Yes, Central is well-known for its challenging road cycling routes, with 133 difficult tours available. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890794716/ascent-to-the-port-of-tudons-and-confrides-circular-from-benidorm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the port of Tudons and Confrides — Circular from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890759210/puerto-de-confrides-and-coll-de-rates-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Confrides and Coll de Rates Loop&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and sustained climbs through mountainous terrain, perfect for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there easier road cycling routes for beginners in Central?
A: Absolutely. Central offers 86 easy road cycling routes, making it accessible for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes often include moderate coastal loops or less demanding inland paths, allowing you to enjoy the scenery without extreme challenges.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I find along road cycling routes in Central?
A: Many routes in Central offer breathtaking views. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891970996/cabeco-d-or-viewpoint-loop-from-sant-joan-d-alacant" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Viewpoint Loop from Sant Joan d'Alacant&lt;/a&gt; leads to a significant viewpoint. Additionally, routes through mountain passes like Coll de Rates provide panoramic vistas of the surrounding valleys and coastline.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural features to explore near the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, Central's routes are often close to fascinating landmarks and natural features. You can explore various caves, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de Bèrnia&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3929236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cremat Cave&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by notable climbs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3935053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Cova del Canelobre&lt;/a&gt;, offering both a physical challenge and a chance to see unique geological formations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Central?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Central, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 700 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from coastal roads to challenging mountain passes, and the well-maintained routes that offer both endurance training and scenic rides. The variety of options for different ability levels is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Central?
A: Yes, Central features many excellent circular road cycling routes. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890794716/ascent-to-the-port-of-tudons-and-confrides-circular-from-benidorm" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the port of Tudons and Confrides — Circular from Benidorm&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891970996/cabeco-d-or-viewpoint-loop-from-sant-joan-d-alacant" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeçó d'Or Viewpoint Loop from Sant Joan d'Alacant&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e891025410/serra-gelada-loop-from-villajoyosa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Gelada Loop from Villajoyosa&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient starting and ending points.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Central?
A: Given Central's Mediterranean climate, the best time for road biking is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for cycling, and the weather is generally stable, allowing you to fully enjoy the coastal and mountain routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in Central?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, Central offers several routes that cover significant distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890813102/carrasqueta-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carrasqueta Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is over 100 km (62 miles) long, providing an excellent challenge for long-distance cyclists.

Q: Can I find coastal road cycling routes in Central?
A: Absolutely. Central's road cycling is defined by its stunning coastal roads. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e892046371/la-marina-baja-along-the-coast-tour-from-altea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Marina Baja along the coast — Tour from Altea&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890996454/alicante-elche-coastal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alicante - Elche Coastal Loop&lt;/a&gt; allow you to cycle alongside the Mediterranean Sea, enjoying beautiful sea views and a refreshing breeze.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Alicante — Circular route to Elxe?
faqs.answer: This 57 km racebike route has an estimated moving time of approximately 3 hours. Your actual duration may vary depending on your pace, stops, and fitness level.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is primarily paved and generally easy to ride, making it well-suited for racebikes. While the broader Alicante region has varied terrain, this specific circular route focuses on smooth surfaces for road cycling.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the Alicante — Circular route to Elxe?
faqs.answer: You'll experience diverse scenery, including coastal views, particularly along the Coastal road. The route also passes near the renowned Palmeral of Elche, a vast palm orchard, and offers glimpses of orchards and almond groves. Heading inland, the landscape transitions to greener areas with pine trees.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Alicante, you can typically find parking within the city. Look for public parking areas or consider parking near the city's outskirts to easily access the route's starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Alicante — Circular route to Elxe suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate' difficulty, this 57 km route with around 280 meters of elevation gain is best suited for cyclists with a good fitness level. While the terrain is mostly paved, the distance and moderate climbs might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Alicante and Costa Blanca region is popular for cycling due to its favorable climate. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Alicante — Circular route to Elxe?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on paved roads, which are generally accessible, specific regulations for dogs can vary. If you plan to stop at any natural areas or parks along the way, always check local signage for leash requirements or restrictions. For the cycling portion itself, dogs would need to be safely transported, for example, in a bike trailer.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other established paths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Vuelta al término de Santa Pola (Etapa 1), Camino de Santiago de la Lana de Alicante, Camino de Santiago del Sureste, and the Camí Vell d'Elx, among others.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route, especially within Parc Natural de les Salines de Santa Pola?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. While a portion of the route passes through the Parc Natural de les Salines de Santa Pola, general access for cycling on public roads within the park does not require a fee. Always respect local regulations and protected areas.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities along the route between Alicante and Elxe?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects two significant cities and passes through populated areas, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and other amenities in both Alicante and Elche, as well as in smaller towns or villages along the way. It's always a good idea to carry some water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a racebike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: For this moderate racebike route, essential items include your racebike in good condition, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, water bottles, snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and navigation tools. Sunscreen and sunglasses are also highly recommended due to the sunny climate.
